message:
fix(windows): use dunce for path canonicalization to strip \\?\ prefix

Replaces all std::fs::canonicalize() calls with dunce::canonicalize()
to properly handle Windows extended-length path prefixes (\\?\).

Problem:
On Windows, Path::canonicalize() returns paths with the \\?\ prefix
(e.g., "\\?\C:\Users\...") which breaks string-based path comparisons,
user-facing display, and tools that don't understand extended-length
paths. This was causing issues in path validation, containment checks,
and CLI output.

Solution:
The `dunce` crate (1.0) provides a canonicalize() function that strips
the \\?\ prefix when the path doesn't require it (paths under 260 chars
without special characters). On non-Windows platforms it's a zero-cost
passthrough to std::fs::canonicalize().

Files updated:
- src/cli/commands/info.rs: canonicalize_lossy()
- src/cli/commands/where.rs: canonicalize_lossy()
- src/config/routing.rs: test path comparisons
- src/sync/path.rs: validate_no_git_path, validate_sync_path,
  require_safe_sync_overwrite_path, validate_temp_file_path
- src/validation/mod.rs: SyncSafetyValidator path containment

This ensures br works correctly on Windows without breaking any
existing Linux/macOS functionality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -52,6 +52,9 @@ rich_rust = { git = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/rich_rust.git", rev =
 # TOON format support (sibling project)
 toon_rust = { git = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/toon_rust.git", rev = "788589d" }
 
+# Path normalization (strips \\?\ prefix on Windows)
+dunce = "1.0"
+
 # Utilities
 once_cell = "1.19"
 regex = "1.11"
